programmes.show_description: Join Caroline Munro as she gives a glimpse at the Hammer treats coming up next week.
Everybody loves a baby. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie starts a farming business, but not one that employs a farm or animals.

programmes.show_name: Eastbourne: Sun Trap of the South
programmes.show_description: Charming 60s travelogue inviting us to enjoy the delights of Eastbourne, where the sun always shines!
1949. Drama. Classic tale of a war romance featuring the smash hit music of The Legend of The Glass Mountain. Stars Michael Denison, Dulcie Gray & Valentina Cortese, with vocals from Tito Gobbi.
Mr Meglomania. 1961. Stars Stephen McNally, Robert Harland & Harold J. Stone. Investigative reporters Paul Marino & Jack Flood investigate various cases of industrial corruption. (S1, E08)
Sticks and Stones. Stars John Ericson, Peggy McCay & Mort Mills. British actor David Niven, introduces this 1950s US anthology series. (S1, E10)
The Critter Doctor. 1964. Stars Irene Ryan, Mark Goddard & Russell Collins. Granny gets frustrated when she confuses a house-to-house insecticide salesman with a critter doctor.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Other Film World
programmes.show_description: 1965. A marvelous look at how the film camera is used in science and industry to increase an individual's knowledge.
1954. Comedy. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars Petula Clark, Donald Houston & Brenda de Banzie. Story of a Welsh postman's efforts to bring happiness into the lives of an assortment of people.
1953. Comedy. Penniless couple Roger (Bernard Braden) and Jean (Barbara Kelly) will get £10,000 if Roger can live a sober and profitable life. Starring Reg Dixon and John Laurie.
1961. An intriguing look at the world of lifts, with a study of the problems faced by engineers to get office workers up to the top of tall buildings.
1960. Drama. Stars Norman Wooland, Jane Hylton & Dennis Waterman. An ex-convict kidnaps his son and takes him on a train to Inverness, unaware he is putting his son's life in danger.
1958. Horror. Directed by Henry Cass and starring Sir Donald Wolfit. In the late 19th Century, the anaemic Dr Callistratus is the governor of a brutal penal institution in a remote castle.
1946. Thriller. Stars Oskar Homolka, Derek Farr & Muriel Pavlow. Antique dealer Mr. Heiss must deal with a blackmailer who knows of his criminal past.
The Golden Road. 1976. Stars Richard Easton & Robin Chadwick. Brian tries to change Jane's position on the golden road to Istanbul. The board members cast their votes on the Middle East project.
Death Ride. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Patrick Barr & Humphrey Heathcote. A man is suspected of murdering his wife; Maguire starts investigating, which motivates someone to try to run him over.
1978. Drama. Directed by Don Sharp. Stars Beau Bridges, Robert Powell & Jane Seymour. Lieutenant Faversham dishonours his regiment and loses his fiancée, but can he regain his honour and his love?

programmes.show_name: A Lifetime Ago with The Baim Collection
programmes.show_description: The Mood Man. 1965. A short film of Britain's greatest bandleader Joe Loss and his orchestra.
1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. This episode focuses on a brilliant tribute to Anna Neagle.
21st Anniversary Programme. 1981. Skate fishing, traction engines, gun dogs and a dancing horse all feature in this special compilation celebrating '21 years of gossiping about the countryside'.
1957. The Mail Van Murder. Directed by John Knight. Starring Hy Hazell, Dennis Castle and Gordon Needham. Supt. Hammond hunts down the boss of a gang that stole a mail van. (S1, E23)
Albatross. 1980. Stars Bryan Marshall, Clifford Rose & Pamela Salem. Burton reshapes the company and ''special cargo'' looms. Personal complications arrive for Tony.
1981. Horror. Director: Lindsey C. Vickers. Stars Edward Woodward, Jane Merrow & Samantha Weysom. Ian misses his daughter's concert so he will have to contend with more than just childish sulking.
1962. Sci-Fi. A spectacular meteor storm blinds the world, except Bill. He, and a few others, must battle with flesh eating plants. Stars Nicole Maurey, Howard Keel, Janette Scott & Kieron Moore.
Pair of Charlies. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie gets blown up-and let down. Based on a storyline by Jack Trevor Story.
1978. Sci-fi Comedy. Director: Norman Tokar. Stars Ken Berry, Sandy Duncan & Harry Morgan. A physicist and his girlfriend try to help an extraterrestrial space cat fix his ship and return home.
1949. Comedy. A nitwit investigator (Terry Thomas) goes undercover in a restaurant to catch a gang of crooks.
The Case of the Cunningham Heritage. 1954. Starring: Ronald Howard, H. Marion Crawford & Archie Duncan. Sherlock and Watson investigate a murder, with a young lady as the prime suspect.
1959. The Hotel. Western with Peter Breck. After his brothers are killed in a shootout, a gunfighter decides to hang up his guns and uphold the law by becoming a lawyer.
1937. Adventure. Director: Edward A. Kull & Wilbur McGaugh. Stars Bruce Bennett, Ula Holt & Frank Baker. Tarzan, Martling and Ula retrieve a Mayan idol, leading them into chaos in the jungle!
1955. Starring Ruth Dunning, Edward Evans, Sidney James, Sheila Sweet, Peter Bryant, Nancy Roberts and Vera Day. Comedy based on the hugely successful TV series 'The Groves'.
1957. Crime Drama. A rogue smuggler enters a murky world when his wife is kidnapped. Directed by Maclean Rogers. Starring Hugh McDermott, Jane Hylton and Honor Blackman.
1953. Adventure. Directed by Robert D. Webb. Stars Robert Wagner, Terry Moore & Gilbert Roland. Fierce competition among sponge fishermen leads to a dangerous rivalry between two families.
1964. Enjoy this jukebox film of the wonderful Colette Renaud with her performance of the song Ah Dis Donc!
A fascinating look at the history of the prolific British film and television production company founded by the Danziger brothers and the studios they established to create features and series.
1957. Mystery. Director: Ernest Morris. Stars Kieron Moore, Jane Griffiths & Basil Dignam. Frank Martin is accused of murder and the only woman who can prove his innocence is missing.
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 1. 1974. Stars William Mervyn, John Flanagan & David Ashford. Two mountaineers died in an unsuccessful attempt to scale the Choga Sar in the Himalayas.
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 2. 1974. Stars Keith Buckley, John Flanagan & Kenneth Cope. Captain Bowman claims that Wainwright lied about Nielsen dying in a fall...
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 3. 1974. Stars William Mervyn, John Flanagan & Keith Buckley. Lloyd accuses the group of blaming Wainwright for Bowman's failures that lead to the two deaths.
1951. Adventure. Directed by Phil Karlson. Stars Barbara Hale, Richard Greene & Carl Benton Reid. A love affair between a farmer and an outlaw's daughter develops with bitter-sweet results.
The electrifying Screamin' Jay Hawkins performs the 1957 song Frenzy.
Dover Castle/The Psychic Healer. 1994. Michael Aspel introduces more unusual events, including paranormal activities at Dover Castle and Lorraine Ham, a remarkable healer who works with the NHS.
Full Circle. 1972. Stars Jean Anderson, Jennifer Wilson & Hilary Tindall. A positive future for Hammond Transport seems certain, but conflict within the family continues.
Murder by Error. 1958. Stars Donald Gray, Diana Decker & John Stuart. Vera thinks that her husband is being blackmailed so she calls Saber. An odd solution is found when blackmail turns to murder.

programmes.show_name: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club
programmes.show_description: Episode 26. 1975. Ronnie Hilton sings a number of hits, New World perform 'Kara, Kara' and 'Sister Jane'. David Copperfield, Valentino and Jimmy Marshall also star.
1953. War. Director: Brian Desmond Hurst. Stars Alec Guinness, Jack Hawkins, Muriel Pavlow, Anthony Steel & Flora Robson. British RAF face invasion on the strategic island of Malta during WW2.
The Plastic Eaters. 1970. Stars John Paul, Simon Oates & Robert Powell. Doomwatch faces its first challenge: to stop the spread of a man-made virus which has the power to dissolve plastic.
